content: Have you ever noticed it's getting hotter outside? Maybe you've heard about polar bears losing their homes because of melting ice. This is happening because of something called the greenhouse effect. Imagine you're in a car on a sunny day with the windows rolled up. It gets really hot inside, right? That's because the sun's rays get trapped. Something similar is happening with the Earth. When we drive cars, turn on lights, or use energy, we release bad things into the air, like a kind of blanket that traps the sun's rays and makes the Earth warmer. We can all help by turning off lights when not in use, planting trees, and telling grown-ups to take care of the Earth. Every small action can help keep our planet cool and safe for polar bears and for us!
